additional.context:
## Dieses Dokument

- Titel: Verlagsvertrag Cassel & Company, Limited
- Signatur: SZ-AAP/L13.6
- Datum: 28th day of October 1937
- Sprache: Englisch
- Objekttyp: Typoskript
- Umfang: 2 Blatt; 1 Rückblatt
- Schreibinstrument: Schwarzes Farbband, violette, blaue und schwarze Tinte
- Hand: Stefan Zweig, Carlos Chanfón
- Anmerkungen: Aufschrift „Superseded by outright sale 1949“ von Carlos Chanfón in blauer Tinte, eigenhändige Unterschrift in violetter Tinte, Unterschrift des Verlagsdirektors in schwarzer Tinte

pages.text:
MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT made this 28th day of October 1937
BETWEEN Herrn Stefan Zweig of 49, Hallam Street, London,
W.1, his executors, administrators and assigns (hereinafter
called the Author) of the one part and Cassell & Company
Limited, of La Belle Sauvage, Ludgate Hill in the City of
London, their successors or assigns (hereinafter called
the Publishers) of the other part.

WHEREAS the said Author has written a new and original work
at present entitled:

"MAGELLAN'S GREAT ADVENTURE"

(hereinafter called the said work) the manuscript of which
is in the hands of the Publishers and which they are
willing to publish in the Spring of 1938 at a published
price of about 15/-d. in the first instance NOW IT IS
HEREBY AGREED AS FOLLOWS:

1. The Author hereby grants to the Publishers on the terms
and conditions set forth in this agreement the sole and exclusive
licence to print, publish and sell the said work in book form in
the English language in the United Kingdom of Great Britain, its
Dominions, Colonies and Dependencies (except Canada) and in Ireland,
and on the Continent of Europe (but the Publishers agree that the
Author shall be free to arrange for the publication of the work in
any Continental edition in the English language, such publication
not to take place earlier than nine months after first publication in
England by the Publishers) the rest of the World except Canada and
the United States of America to be an open market for copies produced
in the English language.

2. The Author guarantees that the said work contains nothing
which is a violation of the copyright of any other person or is of
a libellous character and he will hold the Publishers harmless from
all suits, claims and proceedings which may be brought against them
on the ground that the said work is a violation of copyright or that
it contains anything libellous.

3. IN CONSIDERATION HEREOF the Publishers agree to pay the
Author royalty at the following rates on all copies of the said work
sold:

(a). On the Original Edition:
A royalty of Twelve and one-half per cent (12½%)
of the published price of copies sold up to Two
Thousand (2,000); Fifteen per cent (15%) of the
published price of copies sold from Two Thousand
(2,000) and up to Five Thousand (5,000); Twenty
per cent (20%) of the published price of copies
sold from Five Thousand (5,000) and up to Fifteen
Thousand (15,000), and Twenty-five per cent (25%)
of the copies sold over and
above that number.

(b). On copies of the above edition sold Overseas or in
Great Britain for the purpose of Export:
A royalty of Ten per cent (10%) of the price
received on all such copies sold.

(c). On Cheap Editions:
Ten per cent (10%) of the published price of
copies sold.

-1- Initialled by [?].
pages.notes: Typoskript. Am linken Rand handschriftliche Notiz: "Superseded by outright sale 1949". Unten rechts handschriftliches Kürzel.

pages.text:
(d). In the event of a sale of a quantity of
copies of not less than Two Thousand (2,000)
to the Book Society or similar organisation
at a discount of Fifty per cent (50%) or more
of the published price, a royalty shall be paid
of Ten per cent (10%) of the published price.

(e). The Publishers shall pay the Author on
publication of the Original Edition of the
said work the sum of Four Hundred Pounds (£400)
in advance and on account of royalty payable
under this agreement.
pages.notes: Typoskript. Oberer Teil des zweiten Blattes.

pages.text:
(d). In the event of a sale of a quantity of
copies of not less than Two Thousand (2,000)
to the Book Society or similar organisation
at a discount of Fifty per cent (50%) or more
of the published price, a royalty shall be paid
of Ten per cent (10%) of the published price.

(e). The Publishers shall pay the Author on
publication of the Original Edition of the
said work the sum of Four Hundred Pounds (£400)
in advance and on account of royalty payable
under this agreement.

4. Should the Publishers find it necessary to sell off
any surplus stock or remainder of the said work by auction or
otherwise the royalty payable to the Author shall be Ten per cent
(10%) of the net amount realised by such sale. Should such
surplus stock or remainder be sold at less than cost price then
no royalty shall be payable to the Author on copies so sold.

5. The Publishers shall give the Author six presentation
copies of the said work on publication and he shall be entitled
to purchase any further copies he may require for personal use,
but not for resale, at two-thirds of the published price.

6. Accounts for royalty shall be made up half-yearly to
the 30th April and 31st October and shall be rendered and settled
as soon after these dates each year as practicable.

CASSELL & COMPANY, LIMITED.

Stefan Zweig [Unterschrift]

Newman Flower [Unterschrift]

DIRECTOR.

-2-
pages.notes: Typoskript. Vollständige Seite 2 des Vertrags mit Unterschriften.
Rückseite von Blatt 2, durchscheinender Text von der Vorderseite sichtbar. Leer.
Leeres Blatt, vermutlich Schutzblatt oder Rückseite.
Leeres Blatt, vermutlich Schutzblatt oder Rückseite.

confidence_notes: Der Text ist sehr gut lesbar, da es sich um ein sauberes Typoskript handelt. Die handschriftlichen Ergänzungen sind eindeutig identifizierbar.
